debateOfTheWeek.hook: Rice, pork, and pure South Louisiana pride — who really owns the crown this week?

seasonalRotation.note: When the heat hits, the flavor wars start.

items.description: Smoked, steamed, spicy, or classic — every parish swears theirs is best.

contenders.caseFor: Consistent links with rich seasoning and that perfect snap. It's the benchmark people compare everyone else to.
Balanced spice, juicy texture, and cracklins that hit every time. A road-trip stop that's worth the detour.
Flavor-forward and a little bolder than average. If you like your boudin with attitude, this is your move.

allSpots.note: Underrated and climbing fast with locals. Great value and a no-frills vibe.

items.description: Crunchy outside, melt-in-the-middle fat, and the eternal question: salty or pepper-hot?

contenders.caseFor: Golden crunch and reliable seasoning. People grab an extra bag before they even leave the parking lot.
Some call it unbeatable, others say too rich — which is exactly why this debate gets loud at every boil.
Peppery and intense in the best way. Not for the timid, but pure joy for heat lovers.

items.description: Spice level, peel quality, and price per pound — this one can start a family feud.

contenders.caseFor: One of the absolute best on Johnston with bold, balanced seasoning and consistent quality.
Youngsville favorite with great, juicy crawfish and a strong word-of-mouth following.
Rayne institution with serious flavor depth and loyal locals every season.

allSpots.note: Still a frequent go-to on Ridge with dependable boils.
Traditional boil style with no dusting.
Scott favorite that keeps showing up in recommendations.
Popular stop for dine-in crawfish.
Repeatedly called a hands-down winner.
Very tasty according to locals in the thread.
Frequently mentioned seasonal option.

items.description: Warm, boozy sauce or classic sweet cream? The spoon battles are real.

contenders.caseFor: Rich custard body with a sauce that'll make you scrape the bowl.
Decadent but balanced — sweet without going overboard.

items.description: Icing ratio, filling style, and who gets the baby — Acadiana takes this personally.

contenders.caseFor: Classic style done right with pillowy dough and clean cinnamon flavor.
Creative spins and fun fillings that still respect tradition.

items.description: Spread-style chicken salad gets heated fast — chunky vs creamy, spicy vs mild, and where the jalapeño version wins.

contenders.caseFor: 30+ recommendations with a cult-favorite jalapeño version on Albertsons Parkway.
Oil Center variety and fresh-made spicy Erath version made this a runaway community favorite.
Church Point pick with strong support and old-school deli credibility.

allSpots.note: St. Martinville favorite.
Around 10 recs and praised for amazing flavor.
Real chicken, no fillers.
Milder seasoning and includes eggs.
Smoked chicken salad with Sriracha that sells out fast.
Loved when available, but sells out quickly.
Locals say it tastes like boudin.
Original crowd favorite still repeatedly recommended.
Smoked jalapeño version in Breaux Bridge.
Steady local recommendation.
Dependable and crowd-pleasing quick lunch option.

items.description: Peppered strips and smoky chew — glovebox essentials for every road trip.

contenders.caseFor: Robust seasoning and serious smoke depth.
Classic texture with just enough heat.

items.description: From backyard pits to game day grills, this is the hero link.

contenders.caseFor: Excellent snap and seasoning that holds up on the grill.
A bolder blend for folks who want more kick.

items.description: Seafood gumbo wars: shrimp + crab are non-negotiable, oysters are bonus points, and dark roux is sacred.

contenders.caseFor: Runaway winner from the thread with deep dark roux and standout seafood gumbo.
Praised for generous crab meat and consistent seafood flavor.
Pinhook favorite that keeps popping up in local seafood gumbo discussions.

allSpots.note: Carencro staple; duck & andouille gumbo also highly rated.
New Iberia pick: 'closest to my momma's gumbo.'
Rayne recommendation with multiple mentions.
At Vermilionville on Fridays (+ Wednesdays during Lent).
Duson spot praised for roux done right.
Opelousas classic.
Still in the gumbo conversation across Acadiana.
Frequent community mention.
Upscale option that still earns gumbo respect.
Abbeville recommendation.
Washington destination pick.
Mentioned by locals in seafood gumbo thread.
Classic pick that still gets nods.
Previous site favorite kept for continuity.

items.description: Silky gravy, rice ratio, and spice balance — judged hard by locals.

contenders.caseFor: Classic crawfish flavor and smooth texture that never misses.
Some call it the best in town, others prefer thicker gravy — so the argument lives on.

items.description: Crunch factor, juiciness, and seasoning in the crust — no room for mediocrity.

contenders.caseFor: Unexpected sleeper pick with serious crisp and flavor.
Beloved by many, questioned by purists — exactly the kind of debate fuel we need.

items.description: Rice, gravy, and two sides that hug your soul.

contenders.caseFor: Portions are huge and every tray feels like a Sunday kitchen special.
Reliable comfort classics and neighborhood charm.

items.description: Entrée-style salads where grilled or smoked chicken is the star.

contenders.caseFor: Hands-down winner in the thread for flavor, portion, and consistency.
Pepper jelly chicken salad and Asian almond version both got love.
Cobb salad mention came up repeatedly for fresh ingredients.

allSpots.note: St. Martinville recommendation.
Known for smoked chicken salad.
Caesar salad recommendation.
Local entrant mentioned in thread.
Community pick for grilled chicken salad.

items.description: From classic rolls to omakase-style favorites, this list tracks Acadiana's most-loved sushi spots.

contenders.caseFor: Around 25 recs and legendary quality — temporarily closed but still top of mind.
Downtown pick praised for the freshest fish in Lafayette.
River Ranch favorite with great freshness and atmosphere.

allSpots.note: Great by-the-piece option.
Cheaper sushi options with strong support.
Best-kept-secret status; orange blossom roll mention.
Takeout-only favorite on Kaliste Saloom.
Family-owned Broussard spot.
Broussard location with daily specials.
Both locations got shoutouts.
Chef Andrew's spot; pho also praised.
Consistent local sushi option.
New Iberia pick, especially angel roll.
Very underrated according to locals.
Mentioned as a reliable sushi stop.
Regional favorites that still get nods.
Another recurring recommendation.
All-you-can-eat option in Youngsville.

items.description: Authentic taquerias, family spots, and comfort classics — Acadiana's top Mexican picks.

contenders.caseFor: Consistent food, great service, and popular $12.99 fajita specials midweek.
Repeatedly called the actual best for authentic flavor.
Broussard pick with 'never had a bad meal' consistency.

allSpots.note: Frequent go-to for groups and family dinners.
Broussard pick described as VERY good.
Behind Wendy's on Pinhook.
Old-school favorite.
Known for tongue tacos.
University location still highly recommended.

items.description: Tender, rich birria and consommé done right — one of the fastest-rising local obsessions.

contenders.caseFor: The go-to birria recommendation with multiple independent votes.
Mimosa Place location repeatedly praised for authentic birria.
Eraste Landry standout with strong birria feedback.

allSpots.note: Unexpected but frequently mentioned birria option.
Available on select days only.
Home cook at 710 Solomon St on Taco Tuesdays (birria tacos, pizza, ramen, grilled cheese).
Does birria specials at times.
